faqs.question: What causes drain fly infestations in Van Nest homes?
faqs.answer: Drain flies breed in the organic buildup inside drains, pipes, and sewage systems. In Van Nest's older buildings, aging plumbing with accumulated debris creates ideal breeding conditions. Leaky pipes, slow drains, and moisture issues common in Bronx buildings also contribute to infestations.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take in Van Nest?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments in Van Nest take 1-2 hours depending on the severity of the infestation and number of affected drains. Our technicians will inspect all potential breeding sites, treat drains with specialized products, and provide recommendations to prevent future issues. Results are typically visible within 2-3 days.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 drain fly treatment service?
faqs.answer: Our drain fly treatment includes a thorough inspection of all drains and plumbing fixtures, identification of breeding sites, application of bio-enzymatic treatments to break down organic matter, mechanical cleaning when necessary, and a follow-up plan. Pricing varies based on property size and infestation severity.

faqs.question: Are drain fly treatments safe for Van Nest families and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products that are safe for residential use around children and pets when applied correctly. Our bio-enzymatic treatments are non-toxic and work by naturally breaking down the organic material where drain flies breed. We provide clear instructions for any minimal precautions needed during and after treatment.
